diagrams.title: Distributed Memory Architecture
diagrams.description: Architecture showing multiple compute nodes, each with processor, local memory, and network interface card, connected through a high-performance interconnection network. Data movement occurs only through explicit message passing.
elements: Message arrows showing P0→P1, P2→P3 communication
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the fundamental architecture of distributed memory systems where each processor has private memory and communication occurs via messages through the network.

diagrams.title: Hypercube Network Topology (d=3)
diagrams.description: A 3-dimensional hypercube with 8 nodes at corners of a cube. Each node connects to 3 neighbors along the edges of the cube. The binary representation of node IDs differs by exactly one bit for adjacent nodes.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the hypercube topology structure showing logarithmic diameter (3 for 8 nodes) and high connectivity characteristic of distributed memory networks.

diagrams.title: Halo Exchange Communication Pattern
diagrams.description: Two-dimensional grid distributed across 4 processors, showing ghost cell regions (shaded borders) and the halo exchange communication pattern where each processor exchanges boundary values with its 4 neighbors.
elements: Processor domain grid blocks (P0 top-left, P1 top-right, P2 bottom-left, P3 bottom-right)
Arrow indicators showing data flow between neighboring processors
Communication pairs: P0↔P1, P0↔P2, P1↔P3, P2↔P3
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the halo exchange pattern essential for stencil computations in distributed memory, showing how boundary data is communicated before local computation.
